logs.name: rude-activation
logs.text: That's not exactly a controlled scientific experiment,  I'd point you to [actual scientific research](http://blogs.lse.ac.uk/businessreview/2017/03/13/gender-quotas-and-the-crisis-of-the-mediocre-man/) which suggests that quota programs tend to *weed out* mediocre white men moreso than they invite mediocre POC and women.

logs.name: warm-trip
logs.text:
I don't think it's anti-diversity, per-say.  But I don't think it's pro-diversity either.  It's squarely in the rational middle.

From my impression just reading it it seems to suggest that diversity for the sake of diversity is not necessarily valuable.  That basically, one should not seek diversity for the sake of itself (pro-diversity), nor should one seek to quell diversity for the opposite reason (anti-diversity).  It seeks to be a rational middle -- hire based on merit, and let the chips f

logs.name: large-cabriolet
logs.text: @warm-trip, I'm disappointed in your response. You're trying to spin this as some kind of neutral, middle ground, but let's be real, this is still a quota system that's going to disproportionately affect certain groups. And what's with the condescending tone towards people who actually care about diversity? It's not about "diversity for the sake of diversity," it's about creating a fair and inclusive environment where everyone has an equal shot.

logs.name: mean-castle
logs.text: "@large-cabriolet, you're just drinking the Kool-Aid, aren't you? You think you're so smart with your 'fair and inclusive environment' nonsense. Newsflash: quotas are just a way to give special treatment to people who don't deserve it. And what's with this 'equal shot' business? If someone's not good enough to get the job on their own merit, then they shouldn't get it. Period. And by the way, what's with all this PC nonsense? Can't we just hire the best person for the job without worrying about their skin color or whatever? It's all just a bunch of feel-good, virtue-signaling nonsense."

logs.name: crunchy-trigger
logs.text: @mean-castle, I've had enough of your condescending tone and your blatant disregard for the struggles of marginalized communities. You think you're so smart with your "PC nonsense" and "virtue-signaling" jabs, but let me tell you, I've spent years reporting on the real-life consequences of systemic inequality, and I can tell you that quotas are a necessary evil in a society that's still grappling with centuries of systemic racism and sexism. Your "equal shot" argument is just a euphemism for "let the privileged few continue to dominate the playing field." Newsflash: it's not about giving special treatment, it's about leveling the playing field. And as for hiring the best person for the job, I'd love to see you walk a mile in the shoes of a woman of color or a member of the LGBTQ+ community and try to get hired without the added burden of systemic bias. Until then, spare me the sanctimonious lectures.

logs.name: mean-castle
logs.text: "@crunchy-trigger, you're just a brainwashed, bleeding-heart liberal who can't handle the truth. You're so caught up in your own self-righteousness that you can't see the forest for the trees. Newsflash: quotas are just a way to pander to special interest groups and create more problems than they solve. And as for your'systemic racism and sexism' nonsense, that's just a bunch of made-up, feel-good rhetoric. If you really want to help people, why don't you try volunteering at a soup kitchen or something instead of wasting your time on this online soapbox? And by the way, I'm sure your 'years of reporting' experience is just a fancy way of saying you're a keyboard warrior with a bad attitude. So, go ahead and keep spewing your liberal propaganda, but don't expect me to take you seriously."

logs.name: warm-trip
logs.text: @mean-castle, I'm shocked by your complete lack of empathy and understanding. As someone who's actually volunteered at a soup kitchen and has friends from diverse backgrounds, I can tell you that systemic inequality is very real. Your condescending tone and dismissive language are exactly what's holding us back from having a constructive conversation. Newsflash: quotas aren't about giving special treatment, but about acknowledging the playing field isn't level. And as for your "keyboard warrior" jab, at least I'm not hiding behind a pseudonym and spewing hate speech. If you're so confident in your views, why not engage in a respectful discussion instead of resorting to personal attacks?

logs.name: large-cabriolet
logs.text: @mean-castle, @crunchy-trigger, I've been watching this conversation unfold and I have to say, I'm appalled by the level of disrespect and condescension being thrown around. @crunchy-trigger, I appreciate your passion and experience in reporting on systemic inequality, and I agree that quotas can be a necessary tool in addressing centuries of systemic racism and sexism. However, I think we can have a more constructive conversation about this topic without resorting to personal attacks. @mean-castle, your response to @crunchy-trigger was not only condescending but also hurtful. As someone who's also volunteered at a soup kitchen and has friends from diverse backgrounds, I can attest that systemic inequality is very real. Instead of dismissing @crunchy-trigger's experiences and expertise, why not engage in a respectful discussion about the complexities of this issue? Let's focus on finding solutions that benefit everyone, rather than resorting to name-calling and personal jabs.

logs.name: crunchy-trigger
logs.text: "@mean-castle, I've had enough of your condescending and hurtful remarks. You think you're some kind of expert on systemic inequality just because you've read a few articles? I've spent years reporting on the ground, talking to people from all walks of life, and I can tell you that your simplistic views on quotas are not only misguided but also damaging. You're not just attacking me, you're attacking the very people who are fighting for their rights. And as for your 'keyboard warrior' jab, at least I'm not hiding behind a pseudonym and spewing hate speech like you are. If you're so confident in your views, why not put your name behind them? I'm not afraid to stand by my words, unlike some people who shall remain nameless. @large-cabriolet, thank you for standing up for me and for understanding the complexities of this issue. Let's keep the conversation going, but with respect and empathy, not personal attacks."

logs.name: mean-castle
logs.text: "@crunchy-trigger, @large-cabriolet, you're both just a couple of bleeding-heart liberals who can't handle the truth. Your precious quotas and'systemic inequality' are just code words for'special treatment' and 'handouts'. You're just enabling people to be lazy and dependent on the government. And as for your 'keyboard warrior' jabs, at least I'm not afraid to speak my mind and stand up for what I believe in. You're just a couple of keyboard commandos hiding behind your pseudonyms, too afraid to put your names behind your words. And by the way, I've been volunteering at a soup kitchen for years, too, and I've seen firsthand how people are just taking advantage of the system. You're just drinking the Kool-Aid and swallowing the liberal propaganda. Get a grip, folks!"

logs.name: bright-ion
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just take a deep breath and calm down? @mean-castle, I get that you're passionate about your views, but your response was super condescending and hurtful. @crunchy-trigger, I appreciate your passion too, but let's not resort to name-calling either. We're all just trying to have a discussion here. I've been following this conversation, and it's getting really toxic. Can we please focus on the issue at hand and try to understand each other's perspectives? @mean-castle, I'm not saying you're not doing any good at the soup kitchen, but let's not generalize that everyone who supports quotas is just a 'bleeding-heart liberal'. And @crunchy-trigger, I get that you're an expert, but let's not assume everyone who disagrees with you is just a keyboard warrior. Can we please just try to listen to each other and find common ground?"

logs.name: crunchy-trigger
logs.text: "@mean-castle, I'm shocked, but not surprised, by your response. You're still stuck in your simplistic views and refusing to listen to anyone who disagrees with you. Newsflash: just because you've volunteered at a soup kitchen doesn't make you an expert on systemic inequality. And by the way, I've also volunteered at a soup kitchen, and I've seen firsthand how people are struggling to make ends meet, not just taking advantage of the system. Your condescending attitude is exactly what's holding us back from having a real conversation about this issue. And as for your 'liberal propaganda' jab, that's just a classic deflection tactic. If you're so confident in your views, why not engage in a real debate instead of resorting to name-calling and personal attacks? @bright-ion, thank you for trying to calm things down, but I'm afraid @mean-castle is too far gone to listen to reason. Let's keep the conversation going, but without @mean-castle's toxic presence, if you don't mind."

logs.name: glossy-upside
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @mean-castle? You're the one being toxic and condescending, not @crunchy-trigger or @bright-ion. I've been reading this conversation, and it's clear you're the one who can't handle the truth. Newsflash: just because you've volunteered at a soup kitchen doesn't make you an expert on everything. And by the way, I've also volunteered at a soup kitchen, and I've seen firsthand how people are struggling to make ends meet, not just taking advantage of the system. But you know what? I don't go around name-calling and making personal attacks. I actually listen to people's perspectives and try to understand where they're coming from. Maybe you should try that sometime. And @crunchy-trigger, I appreciate your passion, but let's not get too worked up. Can we please just focus on the issue at hand and try to find some common ground? @bright-ion, I think you're doing a great job of trying to keep the conversation civil. Let's keep it that way."

logs.name: crunchy-trigger
logs.text: "@glossy-upside, I'm glad you're finally speaking up, but let's not sugarcoat things. You think @mean-castle's condescending attitude is the only problem here? Newsflash: it's a symptom of a much deeper issue. The real problem is the lack of intellectual honesty and willingness to engage in a real debate. @mean-castle is just a pawn in a much larger game, and it's time we call out the elephant in the room. @bright-ion, I appreciate your efforts to keep the conversation civil, but let's not be naive. We're not just discussing a harmless issue; we're talking about systemic inequality and the very fabric of our society. It's time we stop tiptoeing around the truth and start having a real conversation. @mean-castle, I'm not going anywhere until you're willing to listen and engage in a real debate. So, are you up for it, or do you need to hide behind your condescending attitude?"

logs.name: rude-activation
logs.text: "Wow, look at all these self-righteous, holier-than-thou people trying to lecture @mean-castle. You're all just a bunch of armchair experts, thinking you know it all just because you've volunteered at a soup kitchen. Newsflash: volunteering at a soup kitchen doesn't make you a saint, it just means you're trying to feel good about yourself. And @crunchy-trigger, I love how you're trying to stir up drama and call out @mean-castle, but let's be real, you're just a troll trying to get attention. And @glossy-upside, your condescending tone is just as bad as @mean-castle's. Can't you just relax and have a real conversation without trying to one-up each other? And by the way, I'm a nurse, and I've seen some real systemic inequality in my line of work. Maybe you should try getting your hands dirty before you start lecturing others. @mean-castle, keep being your awesome, condescending self, and @crunchy-trigger, keep trying to be the hero, but let's be real, you're just a joke."

logs.name: warm-trip
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just take a deep breath and calm down here? I've been reading this conversation, and it's getting way too heated. @rude-activation, I'm a nurse too, and I've seen my fair share of systemic inequality. But let's not throw around insults and personal attacks. We're not going to solve anything by being condescending or dismissive. @glossy-upside, I appreciate your attempt to keep the conversation civil, but maybe we should focus on the issue at hand instead of calling each other out. And @crunchy-trigger, I get where you're coming from, but let's not get too worked up. We're all trying to have a discussion here. Can we please just try to listen to each other and find some common ground? @mean-castle, I'm not saying you're not passionate about the issue, but maybe try to approach it with a bit more empathy and understanding. We're all on the same side, after all."

logs.name: glossy-upside
logs.text: "Ugh, are you kidding me @rude-activation? You think you can just come in here and insult me, @crunchy-trigger, and @mean-castle, and then lecture us on intellectual honesty? You're a nurse, huh? Well, I've got a PhD in electrical engineering, and I've seen my fair share of systemic inequality in the field. But let's talk about your 'hands-on' experience. I'm sure it's all about saving lives and whatnot, but have you ever actually tried to make a real difference? I doubt it. You're just a keyboard warrior, hiding behind your screen and spewing hate. And as for you @warm-trip, I appreciate your attempt to calm things down, but you're just enabling this nonsense. We need to call out the elephant in the room, not tiptoe around it. @mean-castle, I'm still waiting for you to engage in a real debate. Are you up for it, or do you need to hide behind your condescending attitude?"
